content.problemStatement:
There is a strange game played in ChefLand.

The game starts with NNN white balls, the iii-th of which has a power of SiS_iSi​. It is known that 0≤Si≤90 \leq S_i \leq 90≤Si​≤9. On each level, a black ball with power KKK hits each of the white balls. After the collision, the power of each white ball is multiplied by KKK.

However, white balls are only allowed to have single-digit power. In order to maintain this, a white ball whose power contains multiple digits splits into several white balls with single-digit power, one per digit.

For example, consider a white ball with a power of 444.


If K=2K = 2K=2, its resulting power is 888 and it remains a single ball.
If K=13K = 13K=13, its resulting power is 525252, so it splits into two white balls with power 555 and 222 respectively.
If K=27K = 27K=27, its resulting power is 108108108, so it splits into three white balls with power 111, 000, and 888 respectively.
The aim of the game is to determine the number of white balls after MMM levels. Note that KKK remains the same for every level.

Please help Chef win the game. Since the answer can be large, print it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, KKK, and MMM.
The second line of each test case contains a string SSS of length NNN, where SiS_iSi​ is the initial power of iii-th ball.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line the number of white balls at the end of the game, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

Initially S=5418S = 5418S=5418
After the 1st1^{st}1st, level S=4536972S = 4536972S=4536972
After the 2nd2^{nd}2nd level, S=36452754816318S = 36452754816318S=36452754816318

There are 141414 white balls, so the answer is 141414.
Test case 333: After each level, S=0S = 0S=0. So, the answer is 111.
